dashboard.live_overview_hint: Check coverage, recording readiness, and the strongest live signal before reading the chart.
dashboard.logging.waiting: Connect sensors to begin a trustworthy run.
dashboard.logging.unassigned: {count} live sensor(s) still need a location.
dashboard.logging.frame_loss: Frame loss is being detected on {count} sensor(s).
dashboard.logging.offline: {count} sensor(s) are offline, so coverage is incomplete.
dashboard.logging.ready: Ready to record with {connected} online sensor(s) and {assigned} assigned location(s).
dashboard.logging.running: Recording is active with {connected} online sensor(s) and {assigned} assigned location(s).
dashboard.matrix_note: Levels use peak-over-floor dB bands (L1-L5), grouped by source type.
dashboard.rotational.title: Rotational Speeds
history.delete_confirm: Delete run "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
history.delete_all_confirm: Delete all {count} run(s)? This cannot be undone.
history.preview_unavailable: Preview is unavailable for this run.
history.findings_pending: Load insights to review the diagnostic summary, strongest location, and evidence.
history.findings_ready: Loaded insights are shown first, with evidence and supporting statistics below.
report.unable_load_insights: Unable to load insights for selected run.
report.no_findings_for_run: No findings generated for this run.
settings.car.hint: Add cars from the library or enter specs manually. Activate a car to use it for analysis.
settings.car.guidance.no_cars: Add a car below to unlock analysis settings.
settings.car.guidance.no_active: Activate a car from the list below or add a new one to unlock analysis settings.
settings.car.delete_confirm: Delete car "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
settings.car.wizard_intro: Use the library when it fits, or branch into manual specs without losing your place.
settings.car.wizard_summary_intro: Your choices stay visible here while the profile comes together.
settings.car.manual_specs_note: Use this branch when the library stops short or you already know the wheel and gearbox measurements.
settings.analysis.hint: These settings apply globally to all cars and control how vibration frequency bands are calculated.
settings.analysis.no_car_selected: Select or create a car in Settings → Car to unlock analysis settings.
settings.sensors.title: Sensors
settings.sensors.hint: Manage sensor names and locations. Default name is the MAC address.
settings.sensors.name: Name
settings.speed.title: Speed Source
settings.speed.hint: Select how vehicle speed is determined. Manual override is only active when "Manual" is selected.
settings.uncertainty_defaults: Defaults use tire wear from 10/32 in to 2/32 in plus safety margin.
settings.validation_error: One or more analysis settings are out of range. Please check your values.
ws.banner.stale: Data may be stale — no updates received recently
spectrum.stale: Data is stale. Waiting for fresh frames.
spectrum.empty: Connected, but no spectrum frames yet.
spectrum.inspector_idle: Hover the chart or tap a sensor chip to inspect a trace.
spectrum.inspector_focus: Focus: {sensor}. Strongest peak near {freq} Hz at {value} dB. Bands: {bands}.
vibration.db_scale_note: dB is measured above background noise floor.
chart.strength.title: Strength over time
settings.update.title: System Update
settings.update.hint: Connect to a Wi-Fi network to update from GitHub. The hotspot will be temporarily disabled during the update.
settings.update.controls_intro: Enter the Wi-Fi network the Pi should use while it downloads and installs the next release.
settings.update.current_status_summary.r: Ready to start once the Pi has temporary Wi-Fi credentials for the update network.
settings.update.current_status_summary.a: Review the operational health notes below before starting an update.
settings.update.current_status_summary.r: An update is already in progress. Keep this page open until the hotspot returns.
settings.update.current_status_summary.s: The last update completed successfully. Review the installed version and timing before running another.
settings.update.current_status_summary.f: The last update did not finish cleanly. Review the issue list and updater log before trying again.
settings.update.current_status_empty: No previous update details are stored yet. Once the first update runs, this panel will keep the latest status, version, and timing details.
settings.update.issues_intro: Recent validation or install problems that could affect the next run.
settings.update.log_intro: Recent updater output from the most recent run.
settings.update.health.label: Operational health
settings.update.health_card_summary.ok: Core services look stable enough for a maintenance action.
settings.update.health_card_summary.warn: There are active warnings. Review them before taking the hotspot offline.
settings.update.health_card_summary.degr: The system is degraded. Resolve the issues below before attempting an update.
settings.update.already_running: An update is already in progress.
settings.update.reconnect_note: Note: The page may disconnect while the hotspot is down. It will reconnect automatically.
settings.update.guidance.prerequisites_i: A calm update page should answer the basics before the Pi leaves hotspot mode.
settings.update.guidance.prerequisites_1: Use a Wi-Fi network with internet access so the Pi can reach GitHub.
settings.update.guidance.prerequisites_2: Make sure you are ready to reconnect after hotspot access pauses for the update.
settings.update.guidance.prerequisites_3: Run updates when no critical recording or support session depends on the hotspot.
settings.update.guidance.outcome_intro: Starting an update triggers a short maintenance sequence on this Pi.
settings.update.guidance.outcome_1: The hotspot pauses while the Pi joins the Wi-Fi network you provide.
settings.update.guidance.outcome_2: The updater checks GitHub, downloads the release, and installs the new package.
settings.update.guidance.outcome_3: The hotspot and web UI return automatically when the update flow finishes.
settings.update.guidance.recovery_intro: Most update interruptions are recoverable from this page.
settings.update.guidance.recovery_1: Use the health and issues panels to see whether the blocker came from Wi-Fi, runtime validation, or package install steps.
settings.update.guidance.recovery_2: If the page disconnects, wait for the hotspot to come back before retrying.
settings.update.guidance.recovery_3: A successful run records the installed version and last completion time here for the next check.
settings.esp_flash.title: ESP Flash
settings.esp_flash.hint: Build and flash firmware from local source on this Pi.
settings.esp_flash.controls_intro: Choose a serial port or leave auto-detect enabled, then start the firmware build and flash flow.
settings.esp_flash.history_intro: Recent flashes stay here so the next operator can see what happened last.
settings.esp_flash.readiness.summary.rea: The Pi can see at least one serial device. Confirm the target and start when the board is ready.
No serial device is detected yet. Connect the ESP32 by USB and refresh before starting.
settings.esp_flash.readiness.summary.run: Flashing is in progress. Keep the board powered and connected until the status returns.
settings.esp_flash.readiness.summary.suc: The most recent flash completed successfully. You can review the last result and flash again if needed.
settings.esp_flash.readiness.summary.fai: The last flash ended with an error. Review the last attempt and logs before trying again.
settings.esp_flash.readiness.summary.can: The last flash was cancelled. Review the current port selection before starting again.
settings.esp_flash.logs_intro: Build, erase, and upload output appears here while the toolchain runs.
settings.esp_flash.logs_idle_body: Start a flash to stream tool output here. If no port is detected yet, reconnect the board and refresh the port list first.
settings.esp_flash.logs_running_body: The job has started. Build and upload output will appear here as soon as the toolchain begins emitting logs.
settings.esp_flash.history_empty_body: Your first successful or failed flash will be recorded here with the target port and result.
settings.esp_flash.guidance.prerequisite: These checks help the page feel deliberate instead of experimental.
Connect the ESP32 over USB, then refresh the port list if it was plugged in after the page loaded.
Leave the board powered and avoid unplugging it while erase or upload steps are running.
Use recent attempts and live logs to confirm whether the last flash finished cleanly before retrying.
settings.esp_flash.guidance.outcome_intr: This page builds firmware from the local source on the Pi and then writes it to the selected board.
settings.esp_flash.guidance.outcome_1: The Pi compiles the current firmware source and opens the chosen serial connection.
settings.esp_flash.guidance.outcome_2: Erase, upload, and verification steps appear live in the flash output panel.
settings.esp_flash.guidance.outcome_3: A completed attempt is added to recent history so the next operator can see the outcome.
settings.esp_flash.guidance.recovery_int: Most failures can be investigated from this page without leaving the workflow.
settings.esp_flash.guidance.recovery_1: If no device appears, reconnect the board, verify the cable, and refresh the port list.
settings.esp_flash.guidance.recovery_2: If flashing fails, review the latest attempt and log output before retrying.
settings.esp_flash.guidance.recovery_3: A new successful flash updates the last-success timestamp so readiness is easier to confirm next time.
settings.wizard.no_gearboxes: No pre-defined gearboxes. Enter specs manually below.
